title: GreenGateway RBAC Policy
description: Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema for the GreenGateway RBAC policy document. Unknown top-level keys are permitted for forward compatibility and will produce a startup warning.

routes.description: Ordered route-to-permission rules. Rules are evaluated in array order, and the first matching rule wins.

rules.description: Ordered direct firewall rules. Rules are evaluated in array order, and the first matching rule wins. This section is additive alongside routes and does not replace route-to-permission rules.

egress.description: Policy-driven outbound egress rules layered on top of EGRESS_ALLOWED_HOSTS and auto-seeded infrastructure endpoint hosts. Host patterns are additive. CIDRs explicitly allow matching private resolved IPs through EGRESS_DENY_PRIVATE_IPS=true; private IPs outside listed CIDRs remain blocked. If ports is non-empty, the destination port must be listed.

rate_limits.description: Ordered rate-limit override rules. Rules are evaluated in array order, and the first matching rule wins. This is an additional constraint layered on top of the always-enforced, pre-authentication RATE_LIMIT_READ_*/RATE_LIMIT_WRITE_* lanes, not a replacement for them: a matching rule applies only to authenticated requests with a validated principal, and the global lanes still apply to every request regardless of whether a rule matches.

methods.description: HTTP methods this rule matches. Empty or ["*"] means any method.

path_prefix.description: Path prefix matched with starts_with, such as /admin or /v1/.

permission.description: Permission required to access a matching route.

enforcement_mode.description: Optional rule-level override for whether deny decisions block or emit authz.would_deny and forward.

principal_matcher.description: Optional principal constraints for a rule. Non-empty fields are ANDed together: roles AND auth_methods AND principal_ids must all match when configured. Within each field, any listed value matches. An empty object or omitted principal matcher means any principal, authenticated or not.

roles.description: Role names this rule matches. Empty or omitted means any role set.

auth_methods.description: Authentication methods this rule matches. Empty or omitted means any authentication method.

principal_ids.description: Exact principal user_id values this rule matches. Empty or omitted means any principal id.

rule_action.description: Direct rules run before, and take precedence over, the routes/permission model: the first matching rule is the sole authority for the request, and routes are never consulted once a rule matches. 'allow' forwards the request unconditionally (no downstream permission check). 'deny' blocks with 403. 'shadow' also forwards the request unconditionally (like 'allow') but records a would-deny observation event instead of an allowed one, for policy-authoring dry runs. Because both 'allow' and 'shadow' bypass the routes/permission model entirely, an overly broad rule (e.g. an unconstrained principal matcher on a sensitive path) can unintentionally grant access that the routes-based permission model would have denied — scope rule principal/path matchers carefully.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for audit and observation attribution. When omitted, live evaluation reports the rule's array index.

methods.description: HTTP methods this rule matches. Empty or ["*"] means any method.

path.description: Absolute path pattern matched against the whole request path. Syntax is segment-based and anchored, never substring-based. Literal segments match exactly and case-sensitively. * matches exactly one non-empty path segment. ** matches zero or more complete path segments. {name} matches exactly one non-empty segment and names the capture for future rule-preview/discovery UI; capture names use ASCII letters, digits, and _, and must start with a letter or _.

hosts.description: Case-insensitive hostname patterns allowed for outbound egress. A pattern without * matches exactly. A wildcard is only valid as the entire leftmost label in a *. prefix, such as *.example.com; it matches any non-empty DNS label prefix ending in .example.com, including foo.example.com and bar.baz.example.com, but not example.com.

cidrs.description: IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR ranges that explicitly allow matching private resolved destination IPs through the private-IP block. These are exceptions to EGRESS_DENY_PRIVATE_IPS, not a global replacement for it.

ports.description: Allowed outbound destination ports. Empty or absent means no policy-level port restriction.

principal.description: Optional principal constraints. Empty or omitted means any principal, authenticated or not.

methods.description: HTTP methods this override matches. Empty or ["*"] means any method.

path.description: Optional absolute path pattern matched against the whole request path. When omitted, any path matches. Syntax is segment-based and anchored, never substring-based. Literal segments match exactly and case-sensitively. * matches exactly one non-empty path segment. ** matches zero or more complete path segments. {name} matches exactly one non-empty segment and names the capture for future rule-preview/discovery UI; capture names use ASCII letters, digits, and _, and must start with a letter or _.

requests_per_second.description: Positive finite token refill rate for matching requests, in requests per second.

burst.description: Positive token bucket burst size for matching requests.
